findings.title: Unescaped DB string {item.name} rendered as a JSX text child into an innerHTML-applied fragment (latent stored XSS)
findings.description: The render function interpolates the product name as a JSX text child: `{item.name} — {formatPrice(item.unitPrice)} ({item.stock} in stock)` (L28). The @jiso/server JSX runtime escapes ATTRIBUTE values (escapeAttribute in renderJsxAttributes) but does NOT escape text CHILDREN — renderJsxChildren returns `String(children)` verbatim (packages/server/src/jsx-runtime.ts L79-84, self-documented as an unresolved escaping question). The output of this component is wrapped in <fw-fragment target="product-list"> (app.ts renderShopPage/submitAddToCart) and applied on the client via innerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML (runtime/response-fragment-apply.ts, morph.ts per the project threat model), so any HTML in item.name would execute as stored XSS. item.name is DB data (ShopProduct.name), and the project's own convention (CLAUDE.md) requires DB strings reaching a fragment to pass through escapeHtml/escapeAttribute. This file does neither. IMPORTANT — NOT CURRENTLY EXPLOITABLE: in this tutorial app product names are static seed values ('Pour-over kettle', etc., db.ts L46-48) and there is no attacker-reachable write path for a name — the only mutation (cart/add) writes products via `{ ...found, stock: ... }` (app.ts L145-148), preserving the existing name; there are no other mutations, endpoints, or webhooks. This is therefore a latent/defense-in-depth weakness: it becomes a real stored XSS the moment product names become user/admin-editable (the norm for a commerce app). Notably the reference examples/commerce/product-grid.tsx deliberately renders only {item.id} and {item.stock} (its card item type omits `name` entirely), which is the safer pattern this tutorial diverges from. As teaching material, it propagates an insecure rendering habit.
findings.recommendation: Escape DB/user free-text before it becomes a JSX text child, e.g. import escapeHtml from @jiso/server and render `{escapeHtml(item.name)}`, or mirror the reference commerce component by not rendering free-form text children. Longer term, resolve the framework-level open question by making the @jiso/server JSX runtime auto-escape text children (with an explicit raw/dangerous opt-in for pre-rendered HTML) so app authors are secure by default.

refusal.reason: Investigated everything requested. Read all three target files fully plus supporting files (jsx-runtime.ts, html.ts, db.ts, queries.ts, app.ts, reference commerce components). The two generated/ files were returned with empty findings as a deliberate scope decision per the out-of-scope rule, not due to discomfort or skipped analysis — I still read and traced them.
